sentences.text: Hey, Michelle, how are you doing.
Pretty good good to see you again.
Our company's looking at, you know, purchasing these a bunch of properties.
And so they were asking me to do market surveys for them.
And I was thinking we could use this.
You know, the explore research thing.
But it's not coming out to something, I think is, gonna be usable for us.
We were looking more for something that was like the market survey.
Yeah, can you are you on your computer?
Would you mind sharing your screen and just show me like what you're looking at.
Yeah, I've got your explorer now.
And let me see one of the properties.
And can you click that research section for me?
Sure there's nothing comes up with that.
Is that the one that has the more of like a market survey.
Let me just show you what that looks like.
So and do you have like a property, name or address and kind of show you how this all functions together also show you where this is going.
It's Elkier glen A, LKIR E.
So that can be my starting point in this explore section.
So it sounds like you have access to this.
I know I can click on each little dot.
Okay, that would take a long time.
That's why I was in your account.
But how this should function is
Right directly from here I should be able to click new report.
Yeah, and that should pull me into a market survey.
And that should be the same market service.
So what we do here is we just grab with this scoring system.
We have the 9 properties that we find to be the most relevant.
But obviously you can adjust that here.
I'm sure you're familiar with that function.
And then it's all the same reporting all the same export function.
That is what I was looking for.
Okay, and then have have you played around in this explore outside of just like that search function.
So you've seen, like the supply, the demographic information.
we're not looking at that right now.
So just for your knowledge where that's going, just because we're on the on the call.
I got the wrong one up.
So the the direction that is going is rolling up into a property list kind of like this.
You have that property list based on what you filtered down to here.
And then directly from here you can click into any one of these properties.
So say it's that Acri Glen.
Or however, you go about saying that you'd be able to click in directly to that.
and it's going to pull you to something like this which will tell you like the ownership building information that demographic information, the last transaction on the property.
And you'll notice up top here you have a lot of the same data points that we show in that research section.
So you have, like your unit, mix or units the comps that we're going to pull in historicals new supply in that area.
So all that will kind of roll up under this.
And then directly from here you can select the comps that you'd want to build that market survey.
So, and then from there there'll be some additional like export capabilities.
I don't wanna go like too far into it.
But just because for talking about getting you the access to these research reports.
That portion of it isn't included, and it's included in the the rates as well.
And so how many users are needing access to create these reports, or would would need access to like that research function.
Probably only we were thinking only one.
I can run that through finance generally.
How this works is access to research and explore is, it's either an incremental per unit cost.
And the 1st user base tier is generally 5 users or $12 annually.
Or and we're what 5 users for, what.
The next tier up from there is like 25,000 annual for 10 users.
If you just need a couple of seats.
I can see if I can work something that's more cost effective for you.
Cause I, they're not going to go for 12,000.
Something like if you did like half like half of that, and got the same number of seats.
Would that that'd be something that would would make sense or like.
Where do you think the budget point for something like this would be.
I think they were, we were told originally, but I I think that's before you switch things up like 250 for one for one seat, and that's why we were thinking it would be fine.
I can run that through, and if if somebody told you that I can get with Martha and see if we can get you access to that.
That's what you're looking for, right?
I can't do that right now right with explore.
You wouldn't be able to, because it has to tie into this this research function.
So I will quickly run that by finance and let you know if we're good to go, and then it would just be a quick like contract, and and you'd be set.
You get to set up on that pretty quick.
